reasoning: Following the major escalation in early May 2025 (Operation Sindoor), India and Pakistan agreed to a ceasefire on May 11, facilitated by US intervention. Historical precedent (such as the 2019 Balakot airstrikes) suggests that after a major retaliatory strike and subsequent internationally-brokered ceasefire, another strike in "Pakistan proper" within a month or two is highly unlikely, as both sides look to de-escalate. While PM Modi warned of future strikes, this was explicitly conditional on new terrorist attacks occurring, serving as a deterrence posture rather than an indication of imminent proactive military operations. The probability of both a major terror attack occurring in June and immediately precipitating a strike within that same month is relatively low. Cross-border shelling might occur, but typically post-ceasefire periods see a lull, especially across the International Boundary (which borders Punjab and Sindh) as opposed to the Line of Control. Therefore, an attack in June 2025 is unlikely.
batch_queries: Will the Indian Armed Forces execute a kinetic attack in Pakistan proper in June 2025?
